heading: Grants for access and persistence
label: 1070c–3a Grants for access and persistence

children.label: A
children.text: carry out activities under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide coordination and cohesion among Federal, State, and local governmental and private efforts that provide financial assistance to help low-income students attend an institution of higher education;
provide need-based grants for access and persistence to eligible low-income students;
provide early notification to low-income students of the students’ eligibility for financial aid; and
encourage increased participation in early information and intervention, mentoring, or outreach programs.

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: A Authorization
children.text: From sums reserved under for each fiscal year, the Secretary shall make an allotment to each State that submits an application for an allotment in accordance with subsection (c) to enable the State to pay the Federal share, as described in paragraph (2), of the cost of carrying out the activities under subsection (d).section 1070c(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Continuation of award
children.label: i Continuation of award
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), if a State continues to meet the specifications established in such State’s application under subsection (c), the Secretary shall make an allotment to such State that is not less than the allotment made to such State for the previous fiscal year.

children.heading: Special continuation and transition rule
children.label: ii Special continuation and transition rule
children.text: If a State that applied for and received an allotment under this section for fiscal year 2010 pursuant to subsection (j) meets the specifications established in the State’s application under subsection (c) for fiscal year 2011, then the Secretary shall make an allotment to such State for fiscal year 2011 that is not less than the allotment made pursuant to subsection (j) to such State for fiscal year 2010 under this section (as this section was in effect on the day before ).August 14, 2008

children.heading: Priority
children.label: iii Priority
children.text: The Secretary shall give priority in making allotments to States that meet the requirements described in paragraph (2)(B)(ii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of carrying out the activities under subsection (d) for any fiscal year shall not exceed 66.66 percent.

children.label: I
children.text: philanthropic organizations that are located in, or that provide funding in, the State; or

children.label: II
children.text: private corporations that are located in, or that do business in, the State.

children.label: I
children.text: philanthropic organizations that are located in, or that provide funding in, the State; or

children.label: II
children.text: private corporations that are located in, or that do business in, the State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The non-Federal share under this section may be provided in cash or in kind, fairly evaluated.

children.label: aa
children.text: room and board; or

children.label: bb
children.text: transportation passes; and

children.label: II
children.text: helps a student meet the cost of attendance at an institution of higher education.

children.heading: Effect on need analysis
children.label: iii Effect on need analysis
children.text: For the purpose of calculating a student’s need in accordance with part F, an in-kind contribution described in clause (ii) shall not be considered an asset or income of the student or the student’s parent.

children.heading: Submission
children.label: A Submission
children.text: A State that desires to receive an allotment under this section on behalf of a partnership described in paragraph (3) shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: i
children.text: A description of the State’s plan for using the allotted funds.

children.label: ii
children.text: An assurance that the State will provide matching funds, in cash or in kind, from State, institutional, philanthropic, or private funds, of not less than 33.33 percent of the cost of carrying out the activities under subsection (d). The State shall specify the methods by which matching funds will be paid. A State that uses non-Federal funds to create or expand partnerships with entities described in subsection (a)(1), in which such entities match State funds for student scholarships, may apply such matching funds from such entities toward fulfilling the State’s matching obligation under this clause.

children.label: iii
children.text: An assurance that the State will use funds provided under this section to supplement, and not supplant, Federal and State funds available for carrying out the activities under this subchapter.

children.label: iv
children.text: An assurance that early information and intervention, mentoring, or outreach programs exist within the State or that there is a plan to make such programs widely available.

children.label: v
children.text: A description of the organizational structure that the State has in place to administer the activities under subsection (d), including a description of how the State will compile information on degree completion of students receiving grants under this section.

children.label: vi
children.text: A description of the steps the State will take to ensure that students who receive grants under this section persist to degree completion.

children.label: vii
children.text: An assurance that the State has a method in place, such as acceptance of the automatic zero student aid index determination described in section 1087ss(c) of this title, to identify eligible low-income students and award State grant aid to such students.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: I
children.text: Leveraging Educational Assistance Partnership Grants; and

children.label: II
children.text: funded by the Federal Government and the State, and, where applicable, other contributing partners.

children.heading: State agency
children.label: 2 State agency
children.text: The State agency that submits an application for a State under shall be the same State agency that submits an application under paragraph (1) for such State.section 1070c–2(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: not less than one public and one private degree-granting institution of higher education that are located in the State, if applicable;

children.label: B
children.text: new or existing early information and intervention, mentoring, or outreach programs located in the State; and

children.label: i
children.text: philanthropic organization located in, or that provides funding in, the State; or

children.label: ii
children.text: private corporation located in, or that does business in, the State.

children.label: I
children.text: serve as the primary administrative unit for the partnership;

children.label: II
children.text: provide or coordinate non-Federal share funds, and coordinate activities among partners;

children.label: III
children.text: encourage each institution of higher education in the State to participate in the partnership;

children.label: IV
children.text: make determinations and early notifications of assistance as described under subsection (d)(2); and

children.label: V
children.text: annually report to the Secretary on the partnership’s progress in meeting the purpose of this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may provide early information and intervention, mentoring, or outreach programs.

children.heading: Degree-granting institutions of higher education
children.label: B Degree-granting institutions of higher education
I
children.text: recruit and admit participating qualified students and provide such additional institutional grant aid to participating students as agreed to with the State agency;

children.label: II
children.text: provide support services to students who receive grants for access and persistence under this section and are enrolled at such institution; and

children.label: III
children.text: assist the State in the identification of eligible students and the dissemination of early notifications of assistance as agreed to with the State agency; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may provide funding for early information and intervention, mentoring, or outreach programs or provide such services directly.

children.heading: Programs
children.label: C Programs
children.text: An early information and intervention, mentoring, or outreach program that is in a partnership receiving an allotment under this section shall provide direct services, support, and information to participating students.

children.heading: Philanthropic organization or private corporation
children.label: D Philanthropic organization or private corporation
children.text: A philanthropic organization or private corporation that is in a partnership receiving an allotment under this section shall provide funds for grants for access and persistence for participating students, or provide funds or support for early information and intervention, mentoring, or outreach programs.

children.heading: Establishment of partnership
children.label: A Establishment of partnership
children.text: Each State receiving an allotment under this section shall use the funds to establish a partnership to award grants for access and persistence to eligible low-income students in order to increase the amount of financial assistance such students receive under this subpart for undergraduate education expenses.

children.label: i
children.text: the average undergraduate tuition and mandatory fees at the public institutions of higher education in the State where the student resides that are of the same type of institution as the institution of higher education the student attends; minus

children.label: ii
children.text: other Federal and State aid the student receives.

children.heading: Partnership institutions
children.label: i Partnership institutions
children.text: A State receiving an allotment under this section may restrict the use of grants for access and persistence under this section by awarding the grants only to students attending institutions of higher education that are participating in the partnership.

children.heading: Out-of-State institutions
children.label: ii Out-of-State institutions
children.text: If a State provides grants through another program under this subpart to students attending institutions of higher education located in another State, grants awarded under this section may be used at institutions of higher education located in another State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each State receiving an allotment under this section shall annually notify low-income students in grades seven through 12 in the State, and their families, of their potential eligibility for student financial assistance, including an access and persistence grant, to attend an institution of higher education.

children.label: I
children.text: information about early information and intervention, mentoring, or outreach programs available to the student;

children.label: II
children.text: information that a student’s eligibility for a grant for access and persistence is enhanced through participation in an early information and intervention, mentoring, or outreach program;

children.label: III
children.text: an explanation that student and family eligibility for, and participation in, other Federal means-tested programs may indicate eligibility for a grant for access and persistence and other student aid programs;

children.label: IV
children.text: a nonbinding estimate of the total amount of financial aid that a low-income student with a similar income level may expect to receive, including an estimate of the amount of a grant for access and persistence and an estimate of the amount of grants, loans, and all other available types of aid from the major Federal and State financial aid programs;

children.label: aa
children.text: meet the requirement under paragraph (3);

children.label: bb
children.text: graduate from secondary school; and

children.label: AA
children.text: that is a partner in the partnership; or

children.label: BB
children.text: with respect to which attendance is permitted under subsection (d)(1)(C)(ii);

children.label: VI
children.text: information on any additional requirements (such as a student pledge detailing student responsibilities) that the State may impose for receipt of a grant for access and persistence under this section; and

children.label: VII
children.text: instructions on how to apply for a grant for access and persistence and an explanation that a student is required to file a Free Application for Federal Student Aid authorized under to be eligible for such grant and assistance from other Federal and State financial aid programs; andsection 1090(a) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: a determination of the student’s financial eligibility at the time of the student’s enrollment at an institution of higher education that is a partner in the partnership or qualifies under subsection (d)(1)(C)(ii);

children.label: II
children.text: annual Federal and State spending for higher education; and

children.label: III
children.text: other aid received by the student at the time of the student’s enrollment at such institution of higher education.

children.label: i
children.text: Has a student aid index equal to zero, as determined under part F, or a comparable alternative based upon the State’s approved criteria in .section 1070c–2(b)(4) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: Qualifies for the State’s maximum undergraduate award, as authorized under .section 1070c–2(b) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: Is participating in, or has participated in, a Federal, State, institutional, or community early information and intervention, mentoring, or outreach program, as recognized by the State agency administering activities under this section.

children.label: B
children.text: Is receiving, or has received, a grant for access and persistence under this section, in accordance with paragraph (5).

children.label: A
children.text: issue the student a preliminary award certificate for a grant for access and persistence with estimated award amounts; and

children.label: B
children.text: inform the student that payment of the grant for access and persistence award amounts is subject to certification of enrollment and award eligibility by the institution of higher education.

children.heading: Duration of award
children.label: 5 Duration of award
children.text: An eligible student who receives a grant for access and persistence under this section shall receive such grant award for each year of such student’s undergraduate education in which the student remains eligible for assistance under this subchapter, including pursuant to , and remains financially eligible as determined by the State, except that the State may impose reasonable time limits to degree completion.section 1091(c) of this title

children.heading: Administrative cost allowance
children.label: e Administrative cost allowance
children.text: A State that receives an allotment under this section may reserve not more than two percent of the funds made available annually through the allotment for State administrative functions required to carry out this section.

children.heading: Statutory and regulatory relief for institutions of higher education
children.label: f Statutory and regulatory relief for institutions of higher education
children.text: The Secretary may grant, upon the request of an institution of higher education that is in a partnership described in subsection (b)(2)(B)(ii) and that receives an allotment under this section, a waiver for such institution from statutory or regulatory requirements that inhibit the ability of the institution to successfully and efficiently participate in the activities of the partnership.

children.heading: Applicability rule
children.label: g Applicability rule
children.text: The provisions of this subpart that are not inconsistent with this section shall apply to the program authorized by this section.

children.heading: Maintenance of effort requirement
children.label: h Maintenance of effort requirement
children.text: Each State receiving an allotment under this section for a fiscal year shall provide the Secretary with an assurance that the aggregate amount expended per student or the aggregate expenditures by the State, from funds derived from non-Federal sources, for the authorized activities described in subsection (d) for the preceding fiscal year were not less than the amount expended per student or the aggregate expenditure by the State for the activities for the second preceding fiscal year.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: i Special rule
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (h), for purposes of determining a State’s share of the cost of the authorized activities described in subsection (d), the State shall consider only those expenditures from non-Federal sources that exceed the State’s total expenditures for need-based grants, scholarships, and work-study assistance for fiscal year 1999 (including any such assistance provided under this subpart).

children.heading: Continuation and transition
children.label: j Continuation and transition
children.text: For the two-year period that begins on , the Secretary shall continue to award grants under as such section existed on the day before , to States that choose to apply for grants under such predecessor section.August 14, 2008August 14, 2008section 1070c–3a of this title

children.heading: Reports
children.label: k Reports
children.text: Not later than three years after , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall submit a report describing the activities and the impact of the partnerships under this section to the authorizing committees.August 14, 2008
